This budget-friendly security package goes for $180 in full, while discounts since then have only dropped the costs to $160, while the single-camera options have gone as low as $40. The deal here starts already down at $160, with the additional $40 savings ($60 total savings) landing things back at the all-time low price that we spotted during last month’s Prime Day event. You’ll also find the solo-camera option down at its $40 low from $50, and even more of this brand’s security deals in the official Amazon storefront here.

While you may see it carrying around a $1,500 MSRP direct from Segway, you can more often find it discounted or starting at Amazon at $1,200, while discounts usually drop it between $1,100 and $1,050, though there was a fall in January to $1,002, as well as some drops further as low as $1,000 – most recently occurring during June’s Prime Day event. Now, you’re getting another chance at Prime Day pricing with the $200 markdown off the going rate (and $500 off the MSRP) for the best price we have tracked in 2026.

Update 6/15: After going out of stock, this deal is live again at Amazon.

If you’re looking to upgrade your turntable or get into vinyl for the first time, the Sony models are a great option in my experience and its 2026 model PS-LX5BT Wireless Bluetooth Turntable is now within $1 of the lowest price Amazon has offered it for at over $100 off.

items.title: Enshittification
items.summary: Bryan, a food broker from Michigan, wasn't sure if he'd be able to make it to urgent care in time. items.title: Sign up for a Costco Gold Star Membership for $65 and get a $50 Digital Costco Shop Card
items.summary: How can you keep costs low when you end up burning through a quarter tank of gas just running errands all week. Instead of going from store to store and loading up in small bursts, it might be easier if you consolidated and got all your shopping done at once. It might sound hard to pull off, but if you might be surprised by what you can find at Costco. And right now, you can get a 1-Year Costco Gold Star Membership and a $50 Digital Costco Shop Card*, and it’s still just $65.

Google's store page for the Pixel 11 has a short video that shows the glowing, color-shifting orb to the one side of the camera bar. The store […]

items.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
items.summary: Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with several other sensitive categories, suggesting Apple is taking a more curated approach to these ads.

items.title: Amazon offering base M5 MacBook Pro at $150 off new list price, M5 Pro/Max up to $400 off
items.summary: The price of the current-generation M5, M5 Pro, and M5 Max lineup has gone up, but if you are looking to land a machine right now, Amazon does at least have some price relief here. The base M5 models are as much as $150 off the new list prices and some of the higher-end 16-inch models are up to $400 off.

items.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
items.summary: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

items.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model, Inkling
items.summary: It's the company's first public proof point after a year and a half spent building AI infrastructure largely out of public view.

items.title: Phone maker OnePlus reportedly plans to wind down US and Europe operations
items.summary: OnePlus could also wind down its operations in India by 2027.

items.title: Heads up! Lexar’s regularly $480 pocket-sized 2TB iPhone SSD is $220 off today
items.summary: We are now tracking a deal at Amazon that drops the Lexar 2TB Professional Go portable SSD in silver down to $249.99 shipped. This particular drive originally launched at $300 but has sold for as much as $480 in recent months amid rising memory prices and limited supply. While today’s deal does not quite match the $230 Prime Day low it recently hit, it is still an excellent price given how SSD deals are so hard to come by these days. You’re still looking at substantial savings here at 47% off, so be sure to grab it before the price climbs again.

items.title: This aluminum magnetic SSD enclosure is also an 8-in-1 hub with 4K HDMI, and it’s just $26 today (56% off)
items.summary: Courtesy of ORICO’s official Amazon storefront, you can now score this 8-in-1 USB-C hub in orange for $26.39 shipped. Be sure to enter promo code M8WJR4L6 during checkout to see the discounted price. This is a newer version of the 6-in-1 model we have featured before, and it typically sells for $60. It dropped to around $45 during the recent Prime Day sale, but the promo code highlighted above takes over 50% off the usual price and knocks another $19 off that previous low. This is the best price we have tracked for the dock since it debuted, and you can learn more about it below.

items.title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
items.summary: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

items.title: Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data
items.summary: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.

items.title: Update your kitchen with Ninja’s 10-in-1 Prestige smart XL oven and 50% savings to its $200 low (Today only)
items.summary: We just spotted Amazon offering the Ninja Prestige Smart XL 10-in-1 Countertop Oven for $199.99 shipped, beating the direct website where it’s listed at its full $400 price. You won’t want to dawdle long on decisions here, as there is a clock counting down to the deal’s end, and there are 11 hours left at the time of writing this. Discounts on this model have gone as low as $200 previously, which is where Amazon’s June Prime Day event landed it. Now, the deal for 50% savings is back, giving you another chance to update your countertops at the best price we have tracked to date. There are plenty more Ninja appliance deals to check out in the official Amazon storefront here.

items.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
items.summary: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.

items.title: Mid-week Android app deals and freebies: ScourgeBringer, Retro Zoo CEO, Final Fantasy Dimensions, more
items.summary: Your afternoon lineup of the best Android game and app deals is now ready, including titles like ScourgeBringer, Retro Zoo CEO, Guardian War: Ultimate Edition, Final Fantasy Dimensions, Tiny Terraces, Beat Workers, and more. We also have a rare deal live today on the official Google Pixel Flex Dual Port 67W USB-C Fast Charger at 25% off, a solid $300 off Pixel 10 Pro, a chance to land Pixel Watch 4 at $192 off, and Google Pixel 10/Pro Pixelsnap cases up to 36% off, but for now it’s all about the apps.

items.title: Pick up EGO’s 56V 15-inch cordless electric POWERLOAD string trimmer bundle back at its $153 low (Reg. $219)
items.summary: Amazon is offering the EGO Power+ 56V 15-inch Cordless Electric POWERLOAD String Trimmer with 2.5Ah battery down at $153 shipped. Down from $219, discounts up until today have only gone as low as $153 on Amazon in past years, with the last time being back during October’s 2025 Prime Day event, while the most recent June Prime Day saw it keeping to its full price. While the deal lasts, you’re getting the first 2026 chance at the largest $66 markdown to the lowest price we have tracked.

items.title: This grade 5 titanium Apple Watch band costs a fraction of many others, now $63
items.summary:
FiNESTONE Grade 5 Titanium Apple Watch Band

Buy for $63 (Reg. $80)

What we love

While there are bigger brands out there that deliver top-notch bands like this, I personally think it’s nice to see smaller brands put some pressure on them to keep prices in check. Today’s markdown delivers a grade 5 titanium band for $63, a price that is anywhere from two to five times less pricey than some of the others out there.

items.title: Tidy up and top off your Switch 2 Joy-Con controllers with this  charging dock down at $17
items.summary:
JSAUX EnergyFlow Joy-Con 2 Charging Dock

Buy for $17 (Reg. $19)

What we love

I used charging docks like this for all of my controllers, right now I have one for a couple sets of Joy-Con 2 controllers and two for DualSense gamepads. It’s really nice to not only have controllers always topped off, but also to have a dedicated place to set them down when I’m done with them.

items.title: Microsoft patches record number of security vulnerabilities, citing its use of AI
items.summary: Microsoft's monthly release of security fixes, dubbed Patch Tuesday, resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across the company's product line, thanks to discoveries with AI.

items.title: Samsung’s Odyssey G9 49-inch 144Hz ultrawide curved gaming monitor just hit a new all-time low at $680 (Reg. $1,000)
items.summary: Woot is now offering Samsung’s 49-inch Odyssey ultrawide gaming monitor for $679.99 Prime shipped. That is down from its $1,000 list price, saving you $320 with a 32% discount. It is currently fetching its full $1,000 price at Amazon, where it has never dropped below the $700 mark. Woot’s offer undercuts that previous low by $20, making this the new best price to beat. Head below for more details.
